summary.brief: Abruzzi v. Maller. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: In May 2011, Barbara Petillo sought treatment from the defendant Paul Maller, her primary care doctor, for an unexplained fever.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Maller ordered a CT scan, among other tests. Based on Petillo's history of lymphoma and the imaging results, which showed suspicious masses in her abdomen and lung, Maller referred Petillo to the defendant Alexander Hindenburg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hindenburg examined Petillo and thereafter ordered a series of additional imaging tests and biopsies.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Upon her admission to the hospital, blood cultures showed that she had a bacterial infection that developed into endocarditis, an infection affecting the heart.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The judgment, upon the order, is in favor of the defendants Alexander Hindenburg and Winthrop Oncology Hematology Associates, P.C., and against the plaintiff dismissing the complaint insofar as asserted against those defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rothman opined that it was appropriate for Hindenburg 'to work up [Petillo] for a possible malignancy,' that the steps Hindenburg took to do so were proper.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Rothman opined that Hindenburg did not depart from accepted medical practice in failing to diagnose endocarditis.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The Hindenburg defendants demonstrated that Hindenburg's duty of care did not extend to the departures alleged in the complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Petillo's internal medicine and infectious disease expert failed to lay the requisite foundation to render an opinion on Hindenburg's actions as a medical oncologist.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Hindenburg defendants demonstrated through their submissions that Hindenburg's role was limited to evaluating whether there was a recurrence of lymphoma or any other cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, the Supreme Court properly granted the Hindenburg defendants' motion for summary judgment dismissing the complaint insofar as asserted against them.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The decedent's experts opined that the decedent's presentation was more consistent with endocarditis than with lymphoma.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The decedent's oncology expert added that if the decedent had been experiencing recurrent lymphoma, as Hindenburg initially suspected, that condition would have weakened her immune system.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is not appropriate in a medical malpractice action where the parties adduce conflicting medical expert opinions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: I would reverse the judgment, deny the Hindenburg defendants' motion for summary judgment dismissing the complaint insofar as asserted against them, and reinstate the complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Dissenting opinion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient experienced fevers for two to three months, initially resolving with antibiotics but then recurring. She has a history of lymphoma treated with chemotherapy in the 1990s.

focused_exam.abdomen: Palpable lymphadenopathy and a small palpable questionable nodule in the left lower quadrant

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Fever of unknown origin with history of lymphoma

requestables.name: CT scan
requestables.description: CT scan of the abdomen and chest

reveal.result_summary: Suspicious masses in abdomen and lung

requestables.name: Blood culture
requestables.description: Blood culture to check for bacterial infection

reveal.clinical_significance: Could have identified bacterial infection leading to endocarditis
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to perform delayed diagnosis of endocarditis

requestables.name: Oncology consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with oncologist to rule out lymphoma

reveal.result_summary: Oncologist unable to definitively diagnose or rule out malignancy
reveal.clinical_significance: Led to further imaging and biopsy
reveal.decision_impact: Did not address potential infectious causes

key_events.event: Initial consultation with primary care doctor
Blood culture performed, revealing bacterial infection

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with recurring fevers and history of lymphoma
decision_points.prompt: Should a blood culture be ordered to investigate the cause of the fevers?

options.description: Order blood culture to check for bacterial infection
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to rule out infectious causes of fever

options.description: Do not order blood culture, focus on ruling out malignancy
options.clinical_reasoning: Focused on oncological assessment due to history of lymphoma

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not order blood culture

expected_action_court.description: Blood culture should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence suggested need to rule out infection

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order blood culture delayed diagnosis of endocarditis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Blood culture would have identified bacterial infection
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed endocarditis and suffered a stroke

key_findings: Hindenburg's duty was limited to oncological assessment
No breach of standard of care in failing to diagnose endocarditis

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Blood culture could have led to earlier diagnosis of endocarditis

factual_timeline.event: Patient sought treatment for unexplained fever
CT scan performed showing suspicious masses
Patient admitted to hospital, blood culture performed

tests_performed.result: Suspicious masses in abdomen and lung

court_findings.finding: No breach of duty by oncologist
